data.title: As a practitioner, I can enrich a non-epic ticket in-place via brainstorm
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Implement the enrich-in-place path — a streamlined brainstorm dialogue that updates the existing ticket's description with structured content without changing its type.
**Why**: Practitioners with a story/task/bug that just needs clearer intent can enrich it quickly without the overhead of epic creation, fidelity review, or scenario analysis.
**Scope**:
- IN: Streamlined Socratic dialogue to clarify intent, update ticket description with structured acceptance criteria, approach summary, and file paths (if resolvable), skip fidelity review/scenario analysis/ticket creation/complexity routing
- OUT: Convert-to-epic path (Story 2), type detection (Story 1)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, selecting "enrich in-place" runs a streamlined dialogue and updates the existing ticket's description with structured acceptance criteria, an approach summary, and file paths if identifiable
  ← Satisfies: "Enrich-in-place path: updates the existing ticket's description with structured acceptance criteria, an approach summary, and file paths"
- When this story is complete, enrich-in-place skips fidelity review, scenario analysis, ticket creation, complexity evaluation, and routing to downstream skills (/dso:preplanning, /dso:implementation-plan) — the enriched ticket retains its original type and is returned to the practitioner without automatic downstream dispatch
  ← Satisfies: "Skips fidelity review, scenario analysis, and ticket creation. The ticket type remains unchanged."
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Verify skip logic correctly bypasses all epic-specific phases without side effects — especially Phase 3 complexity routing which automatically invokes downstream skills
